name: image_crop
description: Crop a specific region of the image for closer inspection. Use this tool when you need to examine fine details like: leaf patterns, textures, or venation; insect body parts (antennae, legs, wings); animal markings or features; small text or labels in the image.

x1.description: Left boundary (0-1, where 0 is left edge)

y1.description: Top boundary (0-1, where 0 is top edge)

x2.description: Right boundary (0-1, where 1 is right edge)

y2.description: Bottom boundary (0-1, where 1 is bottom edge)

steps: Convert normalized coordinates to pixels: px_i = x_i * W, py_i = y_i * H
Compute center and size: cx = (px1+px2)/2, cy = (py1+py2)/2, s = max(px2-px1, py2-py1)
Make square and expand: s' = s * 1.5
Compute crop region centered at (cx, cy) with side length s'
Shift into bounds if crop exceeds image boundaries
Crop and resize to 448x448 using LANCZOS
